purpose:
Provides secure, immutable offsite backups for core banking platforms to protect against data loss, ransomware, and catastrophic site failure.
Ensures recoverability of critical banking workloads independent of primary data centre availability, supporting APRA CPS 230 and CPS 234 expectations for data resilience.
consumers: All core banking customers hosted by Experteq
currentStack:
Primary on-site backups within Experteq data centres
Customer or platform-specific backup implementations
Limited standardisation of immutable or air-gapped offsite copies
constraints:
No consistent offsite backup standard across core banking platforms
Potential dependence on primary data centre availability
Increasing regulatory scrutiny on data survivability and ransomware recovery
targetState:
A standardised, centrally managed offsite backup capability for all core banking platforms.
Backups are stored in an isolated environment (logical and administrative separation) with immutability controls.
Offsite backups are routinely tested and auditable, with clear RPO objectives defined per platform.
targetDrivers:
APRA CPS 230 operational resilience requirements
APRA CPS 234 information security controls
Ransomware and destructive attack resilience
Customer and auditor expectations for independent recovery capability

risks.description: Loss of recoverable data in a site-wide or ransomware incident
Audit and regulatory findings due to lack of independent offsite backups

actions.component: Define Offsite Backup Standards and RPO Requirements
actions.notes: Define minimum backup frequency, retention, immutability, and platform coverage
Evaluate offsite storage, isolation model, and management approach
Implement pilot offsite backups for a selected core banking workload
Document restore procedures and perform verification testing
Progressively enable offsite backups for all core banking customers
